pages.raw_text:
The fact developed at the meet
ing Of the city council last night
that although over two months have
elapsed since Professor w. H. Burr
of New York, employed as consult
ing engineer on the Monroe street
bridge, was in Spokane, the plans
for the bridge as exported by him
have not yet reached the city coun-
Gil.


After Professor Burr concluded
his examination, it was given out
at the engineers office that he had
approved the plans in every partic-
ular. Last night City Engineer Raj
ston reluctantly admitted that Pro-
fessor Burr had offered a few sug-
gestions as to changes" and that
the making of these changes has so
occupied the engineer office that
the plans are not yet finished.


From other sources the informa-
ton comes that Professor Burr fair
ly hacked the old plans to pieces,
and that the new plans will bear
more Of the earmarks of Burr than
those of Ralston.
